title_original: National Reconstruction Fund Corporation backs next generation quantum computing startup Diraq with $20 million equity investment | National Reconstruction Fund Corporation
summary_original: The National Reconstruction Fund Corporation has invested $20 million in Diraq, a Sydney-based quantum computing startup aiming to deliver utility-scale quantum computers. Diraq is among 11 global companies advancing to stage B of the United States Defence Advanced Research Projects Agency’s Quantum Benchmarking Initiative, highlighting its rapid progress. Founded in 2022 as a spin-out from the University of New South Wales, Diraq employs over 70 people and focuses on silicon-based quantum bits using Complementary Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor architecture, targeting millions of qubits by 2031. The investment will support research, product development, and commercialization, with a first quantum computer product expected by 2029. This exemplifies Australia’s commitment to growing its quantum industry and workforce.
